article_meta.title: Media Assistant AG puts Don Lemon 'on notice' over Minnesota church protest

reasoning: The summary remains neutral and attributes charged language to speakers. It avoids adopting the article’s framing, though it emphasizes Democratic criticism more than supporters’ justifications, creating mild slant.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses mostly neutral verbs; ‘denounced’ is acceptable attribution for quoted criticism.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: All contested claims (e.g., electoral impact, voter suppression) are attributed to sources.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: No subjective judgments stated in the model’s own voice.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: Tone is measured and avoids dramatization.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No personal attacks in the model’s voice.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No assertions about motives beyond attributed quotes.
custom_reasoning.slant: Highlights Schumer’s critique but omits supporters’ stated rationale (election integrity, polling), giving opposition slightly more prominence.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Leaves out Thune’s argument about public support and integrity claims present in the article.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: No loaded adjectives used by the model itself.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Some emphasis on internal GOP tensions and opposition; limited mention of supporters’ case.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Treats official and political sources comparably.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Clearly more neutral than the article’s quoted rhetoric, with proper attribution.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Captures major camps (GOP leadership, Democrats, Trump, GOP skeptics) but omits specific pro-bill rationale details.
